pages.title: About Damian | Damian Hinds
pages.meta_description: Damian Hinds was first elected in 2010 as the MP for East Hampshire. Damian lives between Alton and Petersfield with his wife Jacqui and three young children. He is committed to working hard to support the many excellent East Hampshire businesses, charities and organisations. His priorities include protecting village facilities – the remaining shops, post offices and village schools; and bolstering market towns like Alton and Petersfield.
headings: h3: Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire
pages.text_excerpt: About Damian | Damian Hinds Skip to main content Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire Main navigation Home About News Campaigns In Parliament Surgeries, advice and public meetings Contact Tickets for PMQs and tours of Parliament Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire About Damian Tweet Damian Hinds was re-elected to parliament in the General Election on 4th July 2024. He was first elected in 2010 as the MP for East Hampshire, and was re-elected in May 2015, June 2017, and again in December 2019. He is committed to working hard to support the many excellent East Hampshire businesses, charities and organisations. His areas of interest include business, the economy, tackling disadvantage and widening opportunity. In Parliament, Damian has served on the Education Select Committee, and chaired the All Party Parliamentary Groups for Social Mobility and for Credit Unions. Damian was involved in establishing the All Party Parliamentary Group for T Levels, and also the All Party Parliamentary Group for EdTech, and was a member of the Digital, Culture, Media and Sport Select Committee. In 2015 he was appointed Exchequer Secretary to the Treasury, a role which he held until being appointed Minister for Employment in June 2016. Damian was appointed Secretary of State for Education in January 2018, a position he held for 18 months. Damian served as Security Minister at the Home Office from August 2021 to July 2022, and was appointed a Minister of State at the Ministry of Justice in October 2022. He was appointed Minister of State at the Department for Education in November 2023. Following the July 2024 election, Damian was appointed to the shadow cabinet as Education Secretary. He stepped down from the shadow cabinet in November 2024. Damian is currently a serving member of the Culture, Media and Sport Select Committee and is joint chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group on Down Syndrome. Damian’s degree is in politics, philosophy and economics. Before Parliament, he spent 18 years working in the pubs/brewing and hotel industries, in Britain and abroad. Damian lives between Alton and Petersfield with his wife Jacqui and their three children. pages.text_excerpt: Parliamentary activity | Damian Hinds Skip to main content Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire Main navigation Home About News Campaigns In Parliament Surgeries, advice and public meetings Contact Tickets for PMQs and tours of Parliament Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire In Parliament Parliamentary activity Tweet On this page, you can see Damian's latest activity in Parliament, including speeches and written questions to Ministerial departments. Oral or Written Questions Once a month Ministers from each government department answer questions from MPs at the Dispatch Box. There is a limit to the number of questions that can be asked. MPs can also table a written question to the relevant Government department, which are published in Hansard. http://www.parliament.uk/business/publications/hansard/ Adjournment Debate MPs may be able to raise a constituent's issue in a half-hour Adjournment Debate. To get an adjournment debate, MPs must be successful in a ballot of Members of Parliament or have the subject chosen by the Speaker. The debates are usually the last business of the day and a government minister responds at the end of the debate. Petitions Members of Parliament can present a petition to Parliament on behalf of their constituents. The format and wording of the petition need to be in a particular way. For more information or guidance, please visit: Petitions - UK Government and Parliament Damian's activity in parliament Damian is currently a member of the Select Committee on Culture, Media and Sport, is co-chair of the All Party Parliamentary Group on Down Syndrome and is a member of a number of All Party Parliamentary Groups including heritage railways, rural services, children's online safety, hospitality and tourism, haemophilia and contaminated blood, AI and youth employment. He also recently sat on Bill Committees for the Children's Wellbeing and Schools Bill and the IfATE Bill (now Act). This month, Damian has asked the following written questions (click the links below to see the answers from the Government): Written Answer - Department for Health and Social Care (25 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of awarding Dental Schools status to the University of Portsmouth Dental Academy. Written Answers - HM Treasury (23 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of merging the Valuation Office Agency and HMRC when dealing with businesses, in the context of changes to Agricultural Property Relief. Written Answers - Home Office (4 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what recent discussions she has had with Police and Crime Commissioners on tackling vehicle nuisance in residential areas. Written Answers - Home Office (4 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to give powers to the police to tackle illegally modified exhausts for (a) cars and (b) motorcycles. Written Answers - Department for Transport (4 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 8 May to Question HL6929 on Roads: Cameras, if she will issue guidance to (a) police and (b) local authorities on how roadside noise cameras may be used to tackle noise-related anti-social behaviour. Written Answers - Department for Education (3 June 2025) Damian Hinds: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, how much (a) set up, (b) other one-off, (c) food, (d) staffing and (e) other operating cost funding has been allocated to schools for school breakfast pilots in total; and what estimate she has made of the (i) number of eligible children and (ii) average number of breakfasts to be taken per eligible child over the period. pages.title: Surgeries, advice and public meetings | Damian Hinds
pages.meta_description: Damian holds regular advice surgeries in East Hampshire. There are generally two surgeries each fortnight and individual appointments are for 15 minutes.Sometimes dates have to be changed because of other commitments. Appointments are essential. To make an appointment, please call 0207 219 7057.In an emergency, if the dates are too far away, or if you are housebound, I will try to come to you, diary permitting.Before requesting a surgery appointment please do:- Check whether the issue can be sorted direct (see Resources section below),
headings: h2: The role of an MP
h2: Open Q&A sessions / community meetings
h2: Education - Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND)
h3: Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire
pages.text_excerpt: Surgeries, advice and public meetings | Damian Hinds Skip to main content Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire Main navigation Home About News Campaigns In Parliament Surgeries, advice and public meetings Contact Tickets for PMQs and tours of Parliament Damian Hinds MP for East Hampshire In the Community Surgeries, advice and public meetings Tweet The role of an MP Members of Parliament are elected to the House of Commons to represent the interests and concerns of all the people who live in their constituency, whether they voted for them at the General Election or not. They are only able to deal with issues raised by people who live in their constituency, called constituents. MPs consider and vote on legislation and use their position to ask government ministers questions about current issues. They split their time between working in Parliament and working in the constituency. In Parliament, MPs spend their time fighting for the interests of their constituents, attending debates, scrutinising and voting on legislation, and attending meetings. In the constituency, MPs hold advice surgeries for their constituents to come and talk to them about local issues and problems, attend meetings and community events, as well as visiting local organisations and businesses. Members of Parliament are able to help with all matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible. Click here for the full list of government departments, agencies and public bodies. When a constituent writes to their MP, the MP will then write to the relevant department or official or the Minister involved. Many problems are solved in this way. Alternatively, if a constituent is happy for the issue to be made public, an MP can ask an oral or written question, secure a debate or petition Parliament. Surgeries Damian holds regular advice surgeries in East Hampshire on Friday afternoons. Each appointment is 15 minutes long and must be booked ahead of time. To make an appointment, please email the office using the details below. You will need to provide your full name, full address with postcode and contact telephone number, as parliamentary protocol prohibits Damian from acting for constituents who are represented by another MP. Sometimes we may ask you for your date of birth to verify your identity. In line with parliamentary security guidance, you may be subject to a bag search on arrival. Please do try to leave large bags and coats at home where possible as they may not be permitted in the meeting room. You have the option of attending the surgery in person or requesting an online appointment. Before requesting a surgery appointment please do: Check whether the issue can be sorted direct (please refer to the 'when you need help' section of this page). Think about whether we could start to help you more quickly if you phoned or emailed rather than waiting for the next available surgery appointment. Please note that Members of Parliament are not permitted to intervene in any matters of a legal nature and cannot offer legal advice. Members of Parliament can usually only assist with issues that are the responsibility of the UK Parliament. Often the right person to contact will be a local councillor, Citizens Advice or an ombudsman. They are unlikely to be able to assist with private disputes. MPs do not have any jurisdiction over local Council decisions, so please contact your local Councillor (see below) if you wish to have the assistance of an elected representative in pursuing an issue with a local Council. The full list of services provided by Hampshire County Council can be found here . The full list of services provided by East Hampshire District Council can be found here . The full list of services provided by Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council can be found here . Town Council websites are also useful guides to local services: Petersfield Town Council Alton Town Council Councillors Find your District Councillor (East Hampshire District Council) Find
